description_long: This object is a rectangular emergency exit sign with the word 'EXIT' illuminated in red across its translucent front panel. The sign is flanked on both sides by two octagonal, dome-shaped emergency lights, which are likely adjustable to direct light in different directions. The main body appears to be made of a sturdy, white or light gray plastic housing, with the illuminated panel set into the front. The rear of the object is flat and unadorned, designed to be mounted flush against a wall. The overall construction suggests it is both a visual indicator and a source of emergency lighting, commonly found in public buildings. The sign is utilitarian, with a focus on visibility and durability for safety compliance.
description: A rectangular illuminated exit sign with red text and two side-mounted emergency lights in a white plastic housing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the sign and the protruding dome-shaped emergency lights.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the illuminated 'EXIT' text and both emergency lights clearly.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the thickness and emergency lights from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat, featureless back panel designed for wall mounting.
